content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File                                          Date  : 5-10-77

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: Conversation with Marsden on May 6, 1977.

I indicated to him that it was unclear as to who the civil

prosecutor was.  In light of SB 338, he indicated that

he could forgo all legal action against the official in ques-

tion without prejudicing the case.  Therefore, when the dis-

trict attorney clearly becomes the civil prosecutor after

the passage of 338, the D.A. will be able to prosecute the

case.

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

RICHARD W. MARSTON
CITY ATTORNEY

JACK ALLEN
SR. ASST CITY ATTORNEY

MITCHEL B. KAHN
ASST CITY ATTORNEY

450 NORTH CRESCENT DRIVE
BEVERLY HILLS, CA 90210

AREA CODE 213
TELEPHONE 550-4677

City of Beverly Hills

April 7, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Opinion - Interpretation of Govt.C. §91001

Dear Commissioners:

Your opinion regarding the responsibilities imposed by
Government Code Section 91001 is hereby respectfully requested.

The questions I have are as follows: (1) Am I, as the
appointed City Attorney of Beverly Hills, the attorney responsi-
ble for the enforcement of the provisions of the Political
Reform Act of 1974 against a former City Traffic and Parking
Commissioner for his failure and refusal to file the income
and property disclosure statements required pursuant to this
City's conflict of interest code and applicable interest code when such alleged violation
occurred, if at all, in October 1976? (2) Assuming that
Section 6 of S.B.1356 (Chapter 1161 - 1976 Stats.) is now
the law as of January 1, 1977, is there any plan of intention on the part of the Commission to seek further amendment
of Government Code §91001 so that it will again read as it
did whilst intended by the urgency legislation, S.B.1401
(Chapter 594 - 1976 Stats.)?

While the potential conflict of interest about which I am
concerned stems from a matter involving legal ethics rather
than a matter of financial interest (against which Govt.C.
§87100 is directed), I find it ironic that I may be expected
to perform an official act which is a far greater violation
of conflict rules than that act allegedly committed by the
prospective defendant. The District Attorney of this County

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2
April 7, 1977


has already informed me that it is his position that the
enforcement responsibility in this case now rests with my
office.

If these questions are not worthy of your formal considera-
tion, I would deeply appreciate any informal advice or
guidance your staff might offer in this regard. Thank you.

Respectfully,

[signature]
RICHARD W. MARSTON
City Attorney
City of Beverly Hills

RWM:s

cc: Mayor Richard Stone

sections.question: (1) Am I, as the appointed City Attorney of Beverly Hills, the attorney responsible for the enforcement of the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 against a former City Traffic and Parking Commissioner for his failure and refusal to file the income and property disclosure statements required pursuant to this City's conflict of interest code when such alleged violation occurred, if at all, in October 1976? (2) Assuming that Section 6 of S.B.1356 (Chapter 1161 - 1976 Stats.) is now the law as of January 1, 1977, is there any plan or intention on the part of the Commission to seek further amendment of Government Code §91001 so that it will again read as it did while amended by the urgency legislation, S.B.1401 (Chapter 594 - 1976 Stats.)?
sections.conclusion: I indicated to him that it was unclear as to who the civil prosecutor was. In light of SB 338, he indicated that he could forego all legal action against the official in question without prejudicing the case. Therefore, when the district attorney clearly becomes the civil prosecutor after the passage of 338, the D.A. will be able to prosecute the case.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Beverly Hills asks whether he is responsible for enforcing the Political Reform Act against a former Commissioner for failure to file disclosure statements, and whether the FPPC plans to seek amendment of Government Code §91001 regarding enforcement responsibilities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that it was unclear who the civil prosecutor was at that time, but noted that SB 338 would clarify that the District Attorney would become the civil prosecutor and could pursue prosecution without prejudice to prior actions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both an incoming request letter (April 7) and FPPC internal memorandum documenting the response (May 10). No formal formatted CONCLUSION section; conclusion extracted from memorandum text. Some ambiguity in the final advice language. Unclear whether this is the complete formal response or an interim communication.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: (1) Am I, as the appointed City Attorney of Beverly Hills, the attorney responsible for the enforcement of the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 against a former City Traffic and Parking Commissioner for his failure and refusal to file the income and property disclosure statements required pursuant to this City's conflict of interest code when such alleged violation occurred, if at all, in October 1976? (2) Assuming that Section 6 of S.B.1356 (Chapter 1161 - 1976 Stats.) is now the law as of January 1, 1977, is there any plan or intention on the part of the Commission to seek further amendment of Government Code §91001 so that it will again read as it did while amended by the urgency legislation, S.B.1401 (Chapter 594 - 1976 Stats.)?

CONCLUSION: I indicated to him that it was unclear as to who the civil prosecutor was. In light of SB 338, he indicated that he could forego all legal action against the official in question without prejudicing the case. Therefore, when the district attorney clearly becomes the civil prosecutor after the passage of 338, the D.A. will be able to prosecute the case.

embedding.summary: This document comprises an April 7, 1977 request from the Beverly Hills City Attorney to the FPPC seeking clarification on enforcement responsibilities under Government Code §91001 for alleged disclosure statement violations, followed by an FPPC memorandum from May 10, 1977 documenting informal advice provided regarding prosecutorial authority under SB 338.
